commit.message:
Enhance file gallery, permissions, and export functionality (#533)

This pull request introduces improvements to permission handling and
filter management for charts and number cards, as well as enhancements
to file download functionality and UI logic in the Frappe theme. The
main changes are grouped by theme below.

### Permission Handling Improvements

* Updated `check_chart_permissions_and_settings` and
`check_card_permissions_and_settings` methods to accept an optional
`parent_dt_name` argument, enabling more accurate permission checks
based on dashboard context. Now, permission is granted if the report is
permitted or if the item is not part of a dashboard.
(`frappe_theme/controllers/chart.py`,
`frappe_theme/controllers/number_card.py`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b31463e5509e8762327667139c1595cdb274a647f8e5f4c63066515edd15a43fL18-R26)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b31463e5509e8762327667139c1595cdb274a647f8e5f4c63066515edd15a43fL36-R41)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-91421d7afaf3a01cee8c19677ea0f1220d084c58d498591e6dfdcd6a8ccb4fcdL19-R27)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-91421d7afaf3a01cee8c19677ea0f1220d084c58d498591e6dfdcd6a8ccb4fcdL37-R42)
* Modified settings functions to pass the parent document type name for
permission checks, ensuring correct visibility of charts and number
cards in dashboards. (`frappe_theme/controllers/chart.py`,
`frappe_theme/controllers/number_card.py`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b31463e5509e8762327667139c1595cdb274a647f8e5f4c63066515edd15a43fL109-R114)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-91421d7afaf3a01cee8c19677ea0f1220d084c58d498591e6dfdcd6a8ccb4fcdL60-R67)

### Filter Management Enhancements

* Improved filter handling in chart and number card components by
dynamically injecting filter functions into `dt_events` based on the
report or document type, allowing for more flexible and context-aware
filtering. (`frappe_theme/public/js/vue/sva_chart/components/Chart.vue`,
`frappe_theme/public/js/vue/sva_card/components/NumberCard.vue`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-c8aa9f8e1911cc77239f665dcd8ac953d2510e31301d4ea941127666aed95bfbR380-R407)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-c8aa9f8e1911cc77239f665dcd8ac953d2510e31301d4ea941127666aed95bfbR431-R437)

[[6]](diffhunk://#diff-ec302968b8de1a1b58f4aae395de3e0032ea32ff03b8a825bda741c75b0db2d7R187-R193)

### UI and Download Functionality

* Changed the file download implementation in the dynamic HTML component
to open downloads in a new browser tab instead of using a hidden anchor
element, improving user experience.
(`frappe_theme/public/js/custom_components/dynamic_html/App.vue`)
* Added a new API endpoint for downloading files, utilizing Frappe's
file manager utilities for secure file streaming.
(`frappe_theme/apis/file_manager.py`)

### Visibility Logic Fixes

* Adjusted the initial visibility logic for charts and number cards to
show them only if the user has permission, preventing unauthorized
access and unnecessary rendering.
(`frappe_theme/public/js/vue/sva_chart/components/Chart.vue`,
`frappe_theme/public/js/vue/sva_card/components/NumberCard.vue`)
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-c8aa9f8e1911cc77239f665dcd8ac953d2510e31301d4ea941127666aed95bfbL129-R129)

<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Refactor configuration handling via Functional Options
(Dependency Injection)</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> Currently, the `manael` package reads environment variables directly
within its core logic (e.g., `os.Getenv("MANAEL_ENABLE_AVIF")` in
`avifEnabled()`).
> For a modern Go library design, a package should not implicitly rely
on environment variables. Instead, the application entry point
(`cmd/manael/main.go`) should be responsible for reading environment
variables and passing those configurations down to the library.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Introduce an `Options` struct and use the **Functional Options
pattern** to pass configurations to `NewServeProxy()`. This separates
the concerns, makes the library cleaner, and significantly improves
testability since we won't need to mock environment variables inside the
package logic.
> 
> ### Example Implementation
> ```go
> // manael.go
> type ProxyOptions struct {
> 	EnableAVIF bool
> }
> 
> type ProxyOption func(*ProxyOptions)
> 
> func WithAVIFEnabled(enabled bool) ProxyOption {
> 	return func(o *ProxyOptions) {
> 		o.EnableAVIF = enabled
> 	}
> }
> 
> // Update the constructor to accept options
> func NewServeProxy(u *url.URL, opts ...ProxyOption) http.Handler {
> 	options := &ProxyOptions{}
> 	for _, o := range opts {
> 		o(options)
> 	}
> 
> 	return &httputil.ReverseProxy{
> 		Rewrite: func(r *httputil.ProxyRequest) {
> 			// ...
> 		},
> 		ModifyResponse: func(res *http.Response) error {
> 			return modifyResponse(res, options)
> 		},
> 	}
> }
> 
> func avifEnabled(res *http.Response, opts *ProxyOptions) bool {
> 	contentType := res.Header.Get("Content-Type")
> return opts.EnableAVIF && contentType != "image/png" && contentType !=
"image/gif"
> }
> ```
> 
> ```go
> // cmd/manael/main.go
> func main() {
> 	// ...
> 	enableAVIF := os.Getenv("MANAEL_ENABLE_AVIF") == "true"
> 	
> handler := manael.NewServeProxy(upstreamURL,
manael.WithAVIFEnabled(enableAVIF))
> 	// ...
> }
> ```</issue_description>
>

commit.message:
ci: support multi-architecture Docker builds (linux/amd64, linux/arm64) (#1626)

Manael's Docker image was only built for `amd64`, leaving ARM-based
deployments (AWS Graviton, Apple Silicon) without native images.

## Changes

- **`build.yml`**: Added `platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64` to the
`docker/build-push-action` step — the QEMU and Buildx setup steps were
already present but the platform target was never specified
- **`build.yml`**: Fixed a mislabeled step: "Set up QEMU" was
incorrectly using `actions/setup-go`; renamed it to "Set up Go"

```yaml
- name: Build and push
  uses: docker/build-push-action@...
  with:
    platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64
    push: ${{ github.event_name == 'release' }}
    # ...
```

> Note: QEMU-based `arm64` builds with CGO/libvips will increase CI
build time significantly for release jobs — this is an expected
tradeoff.

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->



<details>

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> 
> ----
> 
> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>CI: Support multi-architecture Docker builds
(linux/amd64, linux/arm64)</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> With the increasing adoption of ARM-based cloud infrastructure (such
as AWS Graviton) and Apple Silicon (M-series Macs) for local
development, providing only an `amd64` Docker image is no longer
sufficient. Image processing is a CPU-bound task, and many users want to
deploy Manael on cost-effective `arm64` servers.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Update the GitHub Actions workflow to build and push
multi-architecture Docker images (`linux/amd64` and `linux/arm64`) to
the GitHub Container Registry (GHCR) and/or Docker Hub.
> 
> Since Manael v3.0 relies on CGO and `libvips`, we cannot simply
cross-compile with `GOARCH=arm64`. Instead, we should use Docker Buildx
with QEMU to emulate the target architecture during the build process.
> 
> ### Tasks
> - Update the Docker build workflow (e.g.,
`.github/workflows/docker.yml`).
> - Add `docker/setup-qemu-action` to enable emulation.
> - Add `docker/setup-buildx-action` to enable Buildx.
> - Modify the `docker/build-push-action` step to specify `platforms:
linux/amd64,linux/arm64`.
> 
> ### Example Workflow Snippet
> ```yaml
> jobs:
>   docker:
>     runs-on: ubuntu-latest
>     steps:
>       - name: Checkout
>         uses: actions/checkout@v4
> 
>       - name: Set up QEMU
>         uses: docker/setup-qemu-action@v3
> 
>       - name: Set up Docker Buildx
>         uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
> 
>       - name: Login to GHCR
>         uses: docker/login-action@v3
>         with:
>           registry: ghcr.io
>           username: ${{ github.repository_owner }}
>           password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
> 
>       - name: Build and push
>         uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
>         with:
>           context: .
>           platforms: linux/amd64,linux/arm64
>           push: true
> tags:
ghcr.io/manaelproxy/manael:latest,ghcr.io/manaelproxy/manael:v3.0.0
> ```
> 
> ### Note on Build Times
> Using QEMU for building CGO and installing C-dependencies (`libvips`)
will significantly increase the CI build time for the `arm64` target.
This is a known tradeoff and perfectly acceptable for release
builds.</issue_description>
>

test: add native Go fuzz tests for header parsing and content negotiation (#1621)

Untrusted HTTP header inputs (e.g. `Accept`, `Vary`, `Cache-Control`)
feed directly into parsing logic that could panic on malformed edge
cases. Go 1.18+ native fuzzing lets the toolchain explore these paths
automatically.

## Changes

- **`fuzz_test.go`** (new, `package manael` internal test package to
access unexported functions):
- `FuzzScanAcceptHeader` — arbitrary `Accept` header values through
`scanAcceptHeader`
- `FuzzCheck` — arbitrary method, status code, `Content-Type`,
`Cache-Control`, and `Accept` through `check`; uses
`&http.Request{Method: method}` directly instead of
`httptest.NewRequest` to avoid panicking on invalid HTTP method tokens
before reaching the code under test
- `FuzzIsAPNG` — arbitrary byte sequences through `isAPNG` (empty,
non-PNG, truncated, partial chunks)
- `FuzzIsAnimatedGIF` — arbitrary byte sequences through `isAnimatedGIF`
(empty, non-GIF, minimal LSD, trailer-only, single-frame)
- `FuzzSetVaryHeader` — arbitrary `Vary` values through `setVaryHeader`,
asserting the output `Vary` header is never empty

Each function ships a seed corpus covering real-world inputs, so `go
test` runs the seeds without needing `-fuzz`.

```go
func FuzzIsAPNG(f *testing.F) {
    f.Add([]byte{0x89, 0x50, 0x4E, 0x47, 0x0D, 0x0A, 0x1A, 0x0A}) // valid PNG sig
    f.Add([]byte{})
    f.Add([]byte("GIF89a"))

    f.Fuzz(func(t *testing.T, data []byte) {
        _, _ = isAPNG(bytes.NewReader(data))
    })
}
```

Run with extended fuzzing via:
```
go test -fuzz=FuzzScanAcceptHeader -fuzztime=60s
```

commit.message:
feat: async/await support with yield-from semantics (#38)

* feat: async/await support with yield-from semantics

Add async function compilation to coroutine objects that work with
MicroPython's asyncio event loop.

Key components:
- AsyncEmitter: extends GeneratorEmitter for coroutine code generation
- AwaitIR and AwaitModuleCallIR: IR nodes for await expressions
- Coroutine type with __await__, send, throw, close methods
- Proper yield-from pattern for await (iterate awaitable to completion)

The critical fix: await expressions now use mp_iternext() to drive
awaitables in a loop until MP_OBJ_STOP_ITERATION, capturing the result
from MP_STATE_THREAD(stop_iteration_arg). This correctly handles:
- Awaitables that yield multiple times
- Return value propagation from sub-coroutines
- Multiple sequential awaits in one function

Tested on ESP32-C6 device with asyncio.run() - all 396 device tests pass.

* fix: resolve lint errors (import order, trailing whitespace)

* fix: address Copilot review comments

- Fix mp_make_stop_iteration to store value in _mock_stop_iteration_arg
- Use per-object tracking for async iterator mock instead of global counter
- Remove duplicate _emit_wrapper_signature (inherits from GeneratorEmitter)
- Fix missing comma in coroutine type table generation
- Add blank lines for code style consistency (ir.py, test_compiler.py)
- Update docstrings to match actual implementation (async_demo.py, ir.py)
- Move async/await section out of 'Out-of-Scope' in docs
- Fix blog examples to match actual code (countdown returns int, not str)

* feat: add yield from support to generators

- Add YieldFromIR dataclass for yield from statements
- Implement yield from emission using mp_getiter/mp_iternext pattern
- Add _yield_iter field to generator struct for sub-iterator tracking
- Update IR builder to handle ast.YieldFrom in statements
- Add IR visualization support for YieldFromIR

Examples added:
- delegate_to_list: simple delegation to list
- flatten: nested list flattening with recursive yield from
- chain_iterables: chaining two iterables
- prefix_and_delegate: mixing yield and yield from

Tests: 4 unit tests, 3 C runtime tests, 4 device tests
Benchmarks: yield from is 1.5-1.6x faster than vanilla MicroPython

Also fixes benchmark runner to use mpremote (was incorrectly using python3).

* fix: address second round of Copilot review comments

- Simplify throw method by removing redundant conditional branches
- Add AsyncFunctionDef to body_ast type hint for accuracy
- Update AwaitIR docstring to clarify simple vs yield-from patterns
- Add blank line before summary section in device tests

* Update docs/05-roadmap.md

fix(classifier,worker): move import time to module level

import time was placed inside classify() and generate() function bodies,
violating PEP 8 (E402 equivalent). All stdlib imports must appear at the
module top level. Python's import caching means this was not a performance
issue, but it is an antipattern that obscures module dependencies.

in memory-tools.ts where packed.length is used as a positional cap.

truncating prompts longer than 512 characters. This truncated embedding
was then passed as precomputedVector to runRecallPipeline where the query
was the full e.prompt. The reuse check (textToEmbed === trimmed) would
pass when HyDE didn't transform the text, causing the truncated-text
embedding to be used for vector search instead of the full-text embedding,
degrading retrieval quality for longer prompts.

Fixed by removing the .slice(0, 512) truncation, so the full prompt text
is embedded for both retrieval and topic-shift detection.

When the prompt is empty or whitespace-only, generateAmbientQueries() does not add a message query at index 0. Add ARM64/aarch64 architecture support

Implement full ARM64 port by factoring architecture-specific relocation
types into build-tag-gated files. This enables the loader to work on both
x86-64 and ARM64 Linux platforms without code duplication.

Changes:
- Created loader/reloc_amd64.go with x86-64 relocation constants
- Created loader/reloc_arm64.go with ARM64 relocation constants
- Moved architecture-specific code from loader/reloc.go to arch files
- Updated loader/loader.go to use generic relocation type aliases
- Updated README.md to reflect ARM64 support in requirements and milestones
- Updated ROADMAP.md to mark Phase 7.4 (aarch64 Port) as complete

The calling convention bridge (purego) already supports both architectures
transparently, so no changes were needed there.

All tests pass on x86-64. The architecture is selected at build time via
Go build tags (amd64/arm64).

docs(history): document sendspin CLI → aiosendspin transition as root cause of PA sink problem

v2.0 replaced subprocess sendspin CLI (each had own PA context, isolated)
with in-process aiosendspin BridgeDaemon — all daemons share one process
and one PulseAudio context. PULSE_SINK env var is process-wide, making it
impossible to route different speakers to different sinks within one process.

This was the root cause that required four routing iterations (v2.1-v2.4)
before being resolved by subprocess isolation in v2.5.

Added to architecture table with note that v2.0 itself introduced the problem
that v2.5 ultimately solved.

commit.message:
Implement multi-threaded TLS support with Dynamic Thread Vector

Complete ROADMAP section 7.3 - TLS Support (Medium priority)

Implements:
- Real OS thread ID tracking using syscall.Gettid()
- Dynamic Thread Vector (DTV) with per-thread TLS block storage
- Thread cleanup functionality to prevent memory leaks
- Comprehensive multi-threaded test suite with isolation verification

Changes:
- internal/tls/tls_get_addr.go: Replace pseudo thread ID with syscall.Gettid()
- internal/tls/tls_get_addr.go: Add CleanupThread() and GetModuleCount() methods
- internal/tls/tls_get_addr.go: Enhanced ThreadLocalRegistry documentation
- internal/tls/tls_test.go: Add 5 new multi-threaded test cases:
  * TestMultiThreadedTLSAccess - concurrent TLS access from multiple goroutines
  * TestPerThreadIsolation - verify thread-specific TLS data isolation
  * TestDynamicModuleGrowth - DTV growth as modules are loaded
  * TestCleanupThread - thread cleanup and memory deallocation
- README.md: Update completed milestones to include M7.3 TLS
- README.md: Remove TLS from "Not Yet Supported" limitations
- README.md: Add TLS to library compatibility list
- README.md: Move TLS from upcoming work to recently completed
- ROADMAP.md: Update section 7.3 status from PARTIAL to COMPLETE
- ROADMAP.md: Document multi-threading features and implementation

Testing:
- All existing tests pass (CGO_ENABLED=0 go test ./...)
- 9 total TLS tests (5 new + 4 existing)
- No regressions in complexity or duplication
- Documentation coverage improved from 91.8% to 93.7%
- New functions have low complexity (CleanupThread: 6.2, GetModuleCount: 6.7)

Impact:
- Libraries with PT_TLS segments now work in multi-threaded environments
- Each OS thread gets isolated TLS blocks via DTV
- Proper cleanup prevents memory leaks when threads exit
- Enables support for pthread-heavy libraries

fix: IK solver tip bone + constraint solver FK propagation

Two critical bugs fixed:

1. IK solver (CCD + FABRIK) used bone_count-1 as tip bone, which
   was always the last bone in the skeleton. For a 333-bone humanoid,
   this meant IK solved from bone 332 instead of the actual constrained
   bone. Changed API: bone_count → tip_bone_idx parameter. The
   constraint evaluator now passes ctx->bone_idx (the bone that has
   the IK constraint).

2. Constraint solver had no FK propagation — when a parent bone was
   modified by a constraint (e.g., IK), children's world transforms
   were stale. Added optional local_pose parameter: when provided,
   the solver computes world = parent_world × local before evaluating
   each bone's constraints, ensuring hierarchy changes propagate.

Walk cycle visual test rewritten to use proper pipeline:
- Works in local space (rest_local) instead of directly manipulating
  world-space matrices
- Passes local_pose to constraint solver for FK + constraint evaluation
- c_traj drives forward motion, c_foot_ik.l/r drive foot IK targets
- Hip bob via c_root_master.x local offset

All 101 unit tests pass. All 4 visual tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: rewrite HISTORY.md with detailed architectural evolution

Complete rewrite targeting readers familiar with HA/MA/multiroom:
- Document the fork from loryanstrant/Sendspin-client (detach commit)
- Architecture v0: single-file monolith, single speaker, 10s BT polling
- Feb 28 explosion: multi-device, HA addon, Proxmox LXC, web UI
- Mar 1: stable player UUID, MPRIS MediaPlayer2, group identity in MA
- Four audio routing iterations (v2.1 → v2.5):
  1. Reactive move-sink-input (race condition)
  2. null-sink + loopback (added latency, broke multiroom sync)
  3. Proactive PULSE_SINK env (inheritance problem in single process)
  4. Subprocess isolation per speaker (final solution)
- PA module-rescue-streams correction, re-anchor feedback loop fix
- Modularization: routes/, services/, state.py, config.py
- Keepalive silence stream to prevent BT A2DP auto-disconnect
- Group pause via MPRIS (MA as initiator for correct syncgroup sync)
- MA REST API integration (v2.9.0): group resume via MA
- Persistent MA WebSocket monitor (v2.9.5): real-time now-playing
- Per-group MA cache for multiple syncgroup support
- Architecture transformation table (14 milestones with rationale)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: export dependent discovery rule type and master_itemid

template.get with selectDiscoveryRules:extend does not reliably return
'type' or 'master_itemid' for discovery rules. Enrich them by fetching
those fields explicitly via discoveryrule.get in export_template_raw().

This fixes two compat CI failures:
  TestExport::test_export_rich_template_preserves_fields
  TestFullRestore::test_restore_rich_template

Both now pass on Zabbix 6.4 and 7.0.
